batch_summary: Deed [318] (LC Inst# 07500489, Vol. 593, Pg. 328) is the ISSUE-01 HIGHEST PRIORITY instrument. It is a warranty deed with vendor's lien from ALL THREE siblings — Georgia Nell Ennis, Genevieve Dorsett, AND John P. Black — to Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons, conveying all three John Boyd Survey tracts (~42.8 acres) near Tehuacana, Limestone County. This is the same property as deed [24] (Verona E. Black → children, December 30, 1974). The grantors reserved only a 10-year non-participating 1/16 royalty interest (expired January 1985). Consideration was $10 cash plus a $12,800 vendor's lien note with deed of trust to L. L. Dorsett as trustee. ISSUE-01 is RESOLVED: 100% of the Boyd Survey interests (not just the hypothesized 2/3) left the family chain in January 1975. No Dorsett or Ennis estate probate research is needed for the Boyd Survey. The family has zero current interest in the John Boyd Survey.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [318] — Boyd Survey tracts confirmed SOLD to Simmons family (Jan 1975); all three siblings conveyed 100%

operations.description: Update John Boyd Survey Tracts section — tracts confirmed sold to Simmons, not held by Dorsett/Ennis estates
operations.find: Grantees were JP Black's siblings: Genevieve Dorsett (d. 4/5/1999, Tehuacana, Limestone County — Limestone County probate) and Georgia Nell Ennis (d. 1/15/2008, San Antonio, Bexar County — Bexar County probate). **Critical lead (Dorsett/Ennis index session, 2026-03-09)**: LC Inst# 07500489 (Vol. 593, Pg. 328, DEED, 01/27/1975) is a joint deed from both sisters (indexed under both Dorsett and Ennis surnames) to SIMMONS ROY ET UX, recorded 24 days after deed [24]. Roy Simmons was the notary on deed [24]. If this deed covers the Boyd Survey interests, the 2/3 interest transferred to the Simmons family chain in January 1975. Companion vendor's lien release (LC Inst# 07804491, Vol. 630, Pg. 105, 06/13/1978) confirms the 1975 instrument was a sale. **PRIORITY**: Pull LC Vol. 593, Pg. 328 to confirm scope. See ISSUE-01.
operations.replace_with: **✓ SOLD TO SIMMONS — ISSUE-01 RESOLVED [deed [318], PDF CONFIRMED 2026-03-31]**: ALL three siblings (JP Black, Genevieve Dorsett, AND Georgia Nell Ennis) conveyed 100% of all three Boyd Survey tracts to Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons via warranty deed with vendor's lien (LC Inst# 07500489, Vol. 593, Pg. 328, filed Jan 22, 1975, recorded Jan 27, 1975) — 24 days after deed [24]. Roy Simmons was the notary on deed [24]. Consideration: $10.00 + $12,800 vendor's lien note (deed of trust to L. L. Dorsett, Trustee; vendor's lien released 06/13/1978, LC Inst# 07804491, Vol. 630, Pg. 105). Grantors reserved a 10-year 1/16 non-participating royalty interest only — **expired January 1985**. Boyd Survey tracts are **completely out of the Black family chain** since January 1975. No Dorsett or Ennis estate probate research is needed for these tracts. The family has zero current interest in the John Boyd Survey.

operations.description: Replace the entire Boyd Survey mineral section with resolved status
operations.find:
### John Boyd Survey Tracts (~42.8 acres, Limestone — 3 tracts near Tehuacana)
- Surface owner: **UNCERTAIN — Ennis estate and Dorsett estate hold 2/3 interest outstanding**
- Mineral owner: UNCERTAIN for same reason
- Interest breakdown per Deed [24] (1974-12-30):
  - JP Black: 1/3 undivided (~14.27 ac equivalent) — passed into trust chain or Olena's estate; disposition uncertain
  - Genevieve Dorsett (JP's sister, d. 4/5/1999, Tehuacana, Limestone County): 1/3 undivided — passed to her estate; **search Limestone County probate records**
  - Georgia Nell Ennis (JP's sister, d. 1/15/2008, San Antonio, Bexar County): 1/3 undivided — passed to her estate; **search Bexar County probate records**
- Tracts: First Tract 16.80 ac, Second Tract 25.00 ac, Third Tract ~1.00 ac less 100×100 ft lot
- **Upstream chain confirmed (deeds [312]+[311], 1959)**: JT Black (d. 1/30/1959) → heirs by intestacy → deed [311] gift deed (2/9/1959) → Verona E. Black. **No mineral reservation in [311]** — full fee simple conveyance of all JT Black estate interests. Verona received 100% surface + minerals.
- See ISSUE-01
operations.replace_with:
### John Boyd Survey Tracts (~42.8 acres, Limestone — 3 tracts near Tehuacana) — SOLD; NOT FAMILY PROPERTY
- Surface owner: **Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons** (or their successors) — per deed [318] (Jan 18, 1975)
- Mineral owner: **Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons** (or their successors) — 10-year 1/16 NPRI expired Jan 1985; Simmons hold 100% since expiration
- **ISSUE-01 RESOLVED [deed [318], PDF CONFIRMED 2026-03-31]**: ALL three siblings (JP Black, Genevieve Dorsett, Georgia Nell Ennis) conveyed 100% of all three Boyd Survey tracts to Roy and Virginia Simmons via warranty deed with vendor's lien (LC Inst# 07500489, Vol. 593, Pg. 328, Jan 18, 1975). $10 + $12,800 vendor's lien note; deed of trust to L. L. Dorsett, Trustee. Vendor's lien released 06/13/1978 (LC Inst# 07804491). Only reservation: 10-year 1/16 non-participating royalty — **expired January 1985**. Family has zero current interest.
- Chain: JT Black (d. 1/30/1959) → heirs → deed [311] gift deed (1959) → Verona E. Black → deed [24] (Dec 30, 1974) → JP Black + Dorsett + Ennis (1/3 each) → deed [318] (Jan 18, 1975) → **Simmons (100%)**
- Tracts: First Tract 16.80 ac, Second Tract 25.00 ac, Third Tract ~1.00 ac less 100×100 ft lot
- No further action required

operations.replace_with: - **Status**: **RESOLVED** (deed [318], PDF CONFIRMED 2026-03-31). LC Inst# 07500489 (Vol. 593, Pg. 328) is a WARRANTY DEED WITH VENDOR'S LIEN from **ALL THREE siblings** (Georgia Nell Ennis, Genevieve Dorsett, AND John P. Black) → Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons. Property: ALL three John Boyd Survey tracts (~42.8 ac) — identical tracts to deed [24]. Consideration: $10 + $12,800 vendor's lien note; deed of trust to L. L. Dorsett, Trustee. Only reservation: 10-year 1/16 non-participating royalty interest (expired January 1985). Filed Jan 22, 1975; recorded Jan 27, 1975; Dena Pruitt, County Clerk; Starlet Ross, Deputy. **100% of Boyd Survey interests left the family chain** — not just the hypothesized 2/3. JP Black's 1/3 never entered the trust chain. Vendor's lien released 06/13/1978 (LC Inst# 07804491). No Dorsett or Ennis estate probate research is needed for these tracts. The remaining ISSUE-43 Dorsett/Ennis pulls (items 12–15) relate to non-Boyd-Survey Dorsett family property only and are LOW priority for project purposes.

operations.description: Add deed [318] analysis entry to ISSUE-01, after the deed [314] analysis entry
operations.anchor: - **Deed [314] analysis (2026-03-31)**: Affidavit of Title (LC Pg. 304–305, Doc# 3773, executed 6/8/1965, filed 7/26/1965). Affiants W.D. Bounds and Jeff Bounds attest to Verona E. Black's ownership of 56.8 ac Lipscomb Norvell Survey, Abstract No. 20, Limestone County. **Key genealogical finding**: Property was acquired by **W. E. Black and wife Ida A. Black** more than 40 years before 1965, and "W. E. Black and his children" conveyed to J. T. Black in 1943. This identifies W.E. Black and Ida A. Black as JT Black's probable parents and reclassifies C.E. Black (ISSUE-43 item 4 grantor in the 1943 Boyd chain deed) as a probable sibling of JT Black rather than a parent. ⚠️ The [314] affidavit references the Norvell Survey, while item 4 is labeled "Boyd chain" — the 1943 deeds may be one multi-survey instrument or two separate deeds. Pulling item 4 (LC Vol. 278 Pg. 379) will resolve this and may reveal whether JT Black's Boyd Survey holdings also trace to W.E. Black. **DISPOSED PROPERTY** — Verona sold this Norvell tract to Wilson in 1965 (companion deed at LC Vol. 516 Pg. 307, still unpulled); no bearing on current project holdings. ISSUE-01 remains OPEN — resolution still depends on the Dorsett/Ennis → Simmons deed (ISSUE-43 item 11).
operations.content: - **Deed [318] analysis (2026-03-31) — RESOLVING INSTRUMENT**: Warranty deed with vendor's lien (LC Inst# 07500489, Vol. 593, Pg. 328, executed 1/18/1975, filed 1/22/1975, recorded 1/27/1975). Grantors: Georgia Nell Ennis (Harris County, TX), Genevieve Dorsett (Jefferson County, AL), and John P. Black (Limestone County, TX) — **ALL THREE siblings**, not just Dorsett and Ennis as previously hypothesized from the county index. Grantees: Roy Simmons and wife Virginia M. Simmons (Limestone County, TX). Property: ALL three John Boyd Survey tracts from deed [24] — First Tract 16.80 ac, Second Tract 25.00 ac, Third Tract ~1.00 ac less 100×100 ft lot previously deeded by John T. Black and Grantor. Consideration: $10.00 + promissory note $12,800.00 (vendor's lien retained; deed of trust to L. L. Dorsett, Trustee). **Mineral reservation**: "Grantors reserve for ten years only a 1/16th non-participating royalty interest in any oil and gas leases that may be executed by Grantees" — **EXPIRED January 1985**. After expiration, Simmons family held 100% surface + minerals. Acknowledgments: Ennis and JP Black acknowledged Jan 18, 1975 before Geraldine Fender NP, Limestone County; Dorsett acknowledged Jan 20, 1975 before Lois S. Moore NP, Jefferson County, Alabama (Dorsett relocated from Dallas County to Jefferson County, Alabama between 1959 and 1975). Filed Jan 22, 1975 at 10:50 AM; recorded Jan 27, 1975 at 4:00 PM; Dena Pruitt, County Clerk; Deputy: Starlet Ross; Limestone County. **Key findings**: (a) JP Black was a co-grantor — his 1/3 interest went to Simmons, meaning the Boyd Survey NEVER entered the trust chain or Olena's estate; the 1979 quitclaims ([08]/[09]/[26]) had nothing to convey re: Boyd Survey; (b) Roy Simmons, who notarized deed [24] on Dec 30, 1974, became the purchaser 19 days later — a pre-arranged transaction; (c) L. L. Dorsett (Genevieve's husband) served as deed of trust trustee — confirms he was alive and active in Jan 1975; (d) Genevieve was in Jefferson County, Alabama by Jan 1975 (previously known in Dallas County per 1959 deeds); (e) vendor's lien was released June 13, 1978 (LC Inst# 07804491, Vol. 630, Pg. 105) — note paid off within ~3.5 years; (f) the 10-year NPRI is the ONLY reservation — no permanent mineral interest was retained. **ISSUE-01 RESOLVED**: The Boyd Survey is not family property. No Dorsett estate Limestone County probate search needed. No Ennis estate Bexar County probate search needed. The remaining ISSUE-43 Dorsett/Ennis pull items (12–15) are non-Boyd-Survey Dorsett family instruments (heirship affidavit, family trust conveyances) of no consequence to project title.
